summary: St. Petersburg police arrested an 18-year-old man following a fatal shooting during a gun transaction inside a YMCA bathroom Wednesday, while officers continue searching for a second suspect.

title: Print St. Pete keeps historic letterpress printing alive
summary: At Print St. Pete, artists are slowing down and putting ink to paper the old-fashioned way.

title: DNA technology identifies 3 men in decades-old Hillsborough cold cases: ‘Identities restored'
summary: The Hillsborough County Sheriff’s Office Cold Case Unit, working with the Hillsborough County Medical Examiner’s Office and forensic genetic genealogy experts, identified three men whose deaths remained unsolved for years.

title: US filings for unemployment aid fall to 187,000 last week, fewest since 1969
summary: U.S. applications for jobless benefits tumbled to the lowest level in more than five decades last week as layoffs remain historically low despite global economic uncertainty.

title: The City of Dunedin to consider banning motorized watercraft launching from Dunedin Causeway
summary: The City of Dunedin will consider banning motorized watercraft launching from Dunedin Causeway at a city commission meeting on Tuesday.

title: Road trip season is here: Expert tips to save money on gas, snacks and car care
summary: Road trip season is here. Experts share tips on gas apps, car maintenance, loyalty programs and more to save money this summer.

title: Taco Bell launches $1 deal as questions over lettuce hurt foot traffic
summary: Taco Bell is offering $1 Enchiritos and Nacho Fries for one day after a lettuce-linked cyclospora outbreak hurt store traffic.

title: Rabies alert issued by health officials in Citrus County after confirmed case in stray kitten
summary: Citrus County health officials have issued a rabies alert on Thursday after a confirmed case in a stray kitten in the area of Floral City.

title: Navy says it will no longer release sailors' service history, another restriction on press access
summary: The U.S. Navy is cutting off press access to sailors' service records. Officials cite security threats — but critics say it's a blow to transparency and the First Amendment. What do you think?

summary: 7/22/26 Capitol Update Extended: Inmates Build Tiny Homes to Support Affordable Housing Efforts first appeared on The Florida Channel Inmates in Escambia County are learning carpentry skills while building tiny homes that will be donated to a local nonprofit to help provide affordable housing. 7/22/26 Capitol Update Extended: Inmates Build Tiny Homes to Support Affordable Housing Efforts first app
